frameworks.name: Four-Energy Model
frameworks.description: Performance is governed by four distinct energy types, each with its own renewal mechanics. Physical is the foundation — all others degrade when physical is compromised.
steps: Audit current physical energy: sleep quality, movement, nutrition, recovery cadence
Audit mental energy: identify decision volume, context-switch frequency, deep work hours
Audit emotional energy: map primary drains (conflict, isolation, misalignment) vs fills (purpose, connection, progress)
Audit spiritual energy: assess alignment between daily work and stated values/goals
Identify the lowest-rated type — that is the current bottleneck
Design one intervention per type, prioritized by bottleneck

examples.situation: Productive in the morning but mentally exhausted by 2PM
examples.diagnosis: Mental energy depleted by excessive context-switching and decision volume in AM
examples.intervention: Batch decisions to one 30-min window. Guard AM for deep work only.

examples.situation: Working long hours but feeling empty and unmotivated
examples.diagnosis: Spiritual energy depleted — work lacks alignment with core goals
examples.intervention: Lucifer motivation archaeology: what do you actually want? Reconnect or redirect.

antipatterns: Treating exhaustion as a scheduling problem (it is usually an energy-type problem)
Optimizing time without optimizing energy — produces more output of lower quality
Confusing low emotional energy with laziness
Using stimulants to override physical energy deficits rather than resolving them

frameworks.name: Ultradian Rhythm Protocol
frameworks.description: The body operates in natural 90-120 minute performance cycles. Working with these cycles multiplies output. Fighting them produces diminishing returns and accelerated depletion.
steps: Identify your personal peak window (typically 90-180 min after waking)
Schedule highest-cognitive-demand work exclusively in this window
Structure work in 90-minute blocks with 15-20 minute genuine recovery between
Define 'genuine recovery': walking, breathwork, stretching — NOT screen scrolling
Batch low-cognitive tasks (admin, email, scheduling) in post-peak windows
Track output quality across the day for 2 weeks — data reveals your actual rhythm

examples.situation: Writer trying to produce in 20-minute gaps between meetings
examples.diagnosis: Work structure fighting ultradian cycles — insufficient ramp-up time
examples.intervention: Block one uninterrupted 90-min deep work window daily. Move all meetings to afternoon.

antipatterns: Scheduling creative or strategic work at end of day
Using rest time for passive content consumption (phone, TV) — does not restore cognitive energy
Attempting 3+ hour blocks without recovery — performance degrades after ~90 minutes
Treating all tasks as equivalent energy cost

frameworks.name: Sustainable Intensity Protocol
frameworks.description: High performance requires periodization — alternating sprint and recovery phases. Continuous maximum intensity is a failure of strategy, not a sign of commitment.
steps: Define your sprint cycle: maximum 2-3 weeks of elevated intensity
Schedule one mandatory recovery period every 3-4 weeks (reduced load, not zero)
Identify your personal burnout early-warning signals (irritability, declining output quality, loss of motivation for enjoyable work)
Set a rule: if 2+ warning signals appear, mandatory recovery activates regardless of deadlines
Protect one full rest day per week — non-negotiable, not earned
Review intensity-output correlation quarterly: does more hours produce more results? (Often no.)

temporal_analysis.one_year: Fast progress, but declining decision quality by month 3
temporal_analysis.five_year: Burnout probable, relationships damaged, health costs compounding
temporal_analysis.ten_year: Total output LOWER than 60-hour sustainable pace due to recovery periods required

examples.intervention: 60-hour sustainable pace with weekly rest day. Longer sprint = more total output over decade.

antipatterns: Treating rest as laziness rather than performance optimization
No periodization — continuous maximum effort until collapse
Ignoring early warning signals until forced to stop

frameworks.name: Energy Audit Protocol
frameworks.description: Weekly diagnostic to identify energy drains, fills, and trajectory — before depletion becomes crisis.
steps: Rate each energy type 1-10: Physical, Mental, Emotional, Spiritual
Identify top 3 drains from the past week (activities, people, contexts that reduced energy)
Identify top 3 fills (activities, people, contexts that restored or generated energy)
Ask: Is overall energy trending up, flat, or down over the past 4 weeks?
If trending down: activate recovery protocol before burnout
If flat: identify the highest-leverage drain to eliminate
If trending up: identify what is working and protect it
antipatterns: Skipping weekly audit when busiest — that is precisely when it is most needed
Only tracking output metrics without tracking the energy that produces them
Diagnosing energy problems in crisis rather than in maintenance

prompts.name: Weekly Energy Audit
prompts.template: Run my weekly energy audit. For each type — Physical, Mental, Emotional, Spiritual — rate 1-10 and identify top drain and top fill this week. Then tell me if I am trending toward burnout or toward peak performance, and what one intervention would have the highest leverage right now.

prompts.name: Burnout Risk Assessment
prompts.template: I want to assess my burnout risk. Current state: [describe current energy, sleep, motivation, output quality]. Identify which of the four energy types is most depleted, what my early warning signals are suggesting, and what recovery protocol to activate — without stopping momentum entirely.

prompts.name: Peak Performance Window Design
prompts.template: Design my optimal daily structure for peak cognitive performance. My current schedule: [describe]. My peak energy window appears to be: [describe]. Identify where I am fighting my ultradian rhythm and redesign the day to protect deep work during my highest-energy window.

prompts.name: Intensity Calibration
prompts.template: I have been running at [intensity level] for [duration]. Assess whether this is sustainable and what the 10-year output projection looks like at this pace versus a sustainable alternative. Then give me a sprint-recovery cycle recommendation that maintains progress without burning out.

prompts.name: Drain Identification & Elimination
prompts.template: My energy feels consistently low despite adequate sleep. Walk me through identifying my top 3 energy drains — across physical, mental, emotional, and spiritual dimensions — and give me one elimination or mitigation strategy per drain, ranked by leverage.

examples.title: Founder Pre-Burnout Intervention
examples.context: Startup founder, 3 months of 80-hour weeks, noticing declining decision quality and irritability
examples.analysis: Physical energy depleted (sleep compressed). Emotional energy low (isolation, high stakes pressure). Spiritual energy intact (aligned with mission). Mental energy the acute crisis — decision quality degrading.
examples.intervention: Immediate: enforce 7-hour sleep floor (non-negotiable). This week: eliminate one day of meetings entirely. This month: reduce to 60-hour pace, protect Sunday completely. Review: is output volume actually decreasing at 60 hours, or is this assumption?
examples.outcome_hypothesis: Decision quality recovers within 2 weeks. Total monthly output equivalent or higher due to quality improvement.

examples.title: Knowledge Worker Context-Switch Trap
examples.context: Consultant doing 6+ context switches daily, feeling mentally exhausted by noon
examples.framework_applied: Ultradian Rhythm Protocol + Mental Energy Audit
examples.analysis: Context-switching is the primary mental energy drain. Each switch costs 15-25 minutes of cognitive ramp-up. 6 switches = 90-150 minutes of lost productive capacity daily.
examples.intervention: Group all client work by client, consecutively. No interleaving. Schedule client-facing work in AM peak window. Email and admin strictly 3-5PM.
examples.outcome_hypothesis: Effective deep work hours increase from ~2 to ~4 per day with same clock hours.

vocabulary.definition: Body-level fuel — determined by sleep, movement, nutrition, and recovery. Foundation of all other energy types.
Cognitive fuel for focus, decisions, and analysis. Finite daily supply, depleted by volume and context-switching.
Motivational and relational fuel. Drained by conflict, isolation, meaningless work; restored by purpose, connection, progress.
Alignment fuel — sense that current work serves meaningful goals. Drained by inauthenticity; restored by purpose and contribution.
Natural 90-120 minute performance cycles governing cognitive capacity. Working with these cycles multiplies output.
The period of peak cognitive performance — typically first 90-180 minutes post-waking. Highest-value time that must be protected.
Failure of energy management strategy, not a character flaw. Characterized by depleted physical + emotional + mental energy simultaneously.
Alternating sprint (high intensity) and recovery (reduced intensity) phases. Applied to cognitive performance from athletic training science.
Active restoration of energy — walking, breathwork, sleep, social connection. Distinct from passive rest (TV, scrolling), which does not restore cognitive energy.
Activity, context, or relationship that consistently depletes energy beyond what the activity itself demands.
Activity, context, or relationship that restores or generates energy, often beyond what would be predicted.
The 15-25 minute cognitive ramp-up required after switching between meaningfully different tasks. Invisible but substantial.

sources.title: Deep Work
sources.relevance: Ultradian rhythm protocols, deep work window architecture

sources.title: Why We Sleep
sources.relevance: Physical energy foundation — sleep as performance multiplier

sources.title: Huberman Lab Podcast
sources.relevance: Neuroscience of peak performance, ultradian rhythm research

4_gate_relevance.gate_3_leverage: Energy optimization is highest-leverage intervention when depleted — everything else degrades
4_gate_relevance.gate_4_aligned: Unsustainable intensity destroys long-term compounding — aligned with G1 only when sustainable

4_gate_validation.safe: PASS — no harmful advice; recovery protocols emphasize gradual intervention
4_gate_validation.true: PASS — grounded in established performance science (Loehr/Schwartz, Newport, Walker, Huberman)
4_gate_validation.high_leverage: PASS — energy management is prerequisite for all other performance gains
4_gate_validation.aligned: PASS — serves Vitality need; sustainability mandate serves Legacy/Growth via compound protection
